chunk_summary: This section discusses storage temperatures and time limits for expressed milk, including guidelines for donor milk and milk banking, referencing authoritative protocols and national guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand optimal storage temperatures for expressed breast milk.
Learn time limits for safe storage of expressed milk at various temperatures.
Explore guidelines for donor milk and milk banking practices.
Identify key recommendations from national and clinical protocols.
key_facts: Expressed milk can be stored at room temperature (up to 25°C) for 4 hours.
Refrigerated milk (≤4°C) is safe for up to 4 days.
Frozen milk (≤-18°C) can be stored for up to 6 months for home use.
Thawed milk should be used within 24 hours and not refrozen.
Donor milk storage follows similar guidelines but may include additional screening protocols.
reference_quote: Expressed milk can be stored at room temperature (up to 25°C) for 4 hours, refrigerated (≤4°C) for up to 4 days, and frozen (≤-18°C) for up to 6 months. Thawed milk should be used within 24 hours and not refrozen. (Source: ABM Clinical Protocol #8)
document_key_topics: Time limits for safe milk storage
Guidelines for donor milk and milk banking
